title: Agent Lifecycle Management
description: Onboarding, monitoring, versioning, retraining, and retiring AI agents as enterprise workforce participants. Includes agent registry and performance management.

title: Model Portfolio Management
description: Managing the enterprise's portfolio of AI models: selection, benchmarking, cost tracking, vendor diversification, right-sizing, and deprecation planning.

title: AI FinOps
description: Financial governance for AI operations: inference cost tracking, fine-tuning budgets, infrastructure cost allocation, budget thresholds, and escalation triggers.

title: Continuous Architecture Compliance
description: Automated, continuous compliance checking integrated into CI/CD pipelines. Policy-as-code with automated enforcement at deployment time.

title: Sovereignty by Design
description: Data residency, model provenance, and jurisdictional constraints as first-order architecture design inputs that constrain infrastructure decisions.

title: Enterprise as Code
description: Codifying implicit operating models — workflows, decision rules, governance logic — as executable code readable by both humans and AI agents.

title: Agent Communication Architecture
description: Protocol abstraction layer for agent-to-agent communication, decoupled from specific protocols (MCP, A2A, ACP). The defining architectural bet for 2026–2028.

title: AI Infrastructure Readiness Assessment
description: Structured assessment of compute, network, storage, and integration capacity against planned AI workloads. Only 22% of organisations are currently ready.

title: Agent Risk and Observability
description: Distributed tracing, blast-radius controls, circuit breakers, and cascade failure detection for non-deterministic autonomous agent systems.

title: AI Value Progression Management
description: Framework for planning the platform transition from productivity (phase one) to revenue (phase two) AI, with differentiated KPIs and aligned leadership expectations.
